page: Microsoft Azure D48nds_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dndv6 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture, powered by an Intel processor, and runs under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 4.0 GB per core.

This server type includes 2835 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-throughput disk configurations. As a network-optimized instance, it is designed to handle substantial network traffic, though it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. There are no onboard GPUs or hardware accelerators bundled with this configuration.

The resource density of the D48nds_v6 provides a qualitative balance between compute capacity and memory allocation. The inclusion of a large local disk alongside dedicated CPU resources makes it suitable for workloads requiring consistent processing power and local data caching. Appropriate workloads include database hosting, network-intensive applications, and general-purpose enterprise deployments.
description: Microsoft Azure D48nds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the network-optimized Dndv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio of 4.0 GB per core. For storage, the instance is bundled with 2835 GB of local disk space and supports Premium Storage configurations. It does not include integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile is designed for network-intensive applications, database hosting, and general-purpose enterprise workloads that require dedicated compute resources alongside substantial local storage capacity. The combination of dedicated processing power and high-capacity local storage supports consistent performance for data-intensive operations.
